what_it_does: Defines an inline SVG icon showing a speaker shape with crossed mute strokes, using currentColor stroke styling.
observations: Rendering is stroke-based with fill set to none.
Stroke color is currentColor, so the icon inherits text or parent color.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The first path draws the speaker body and sound source shape.
The second path draws two diagonal crossing strokes to indicate mute.
standouts: Speaker and mute mark are separated into distinct paths.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions: 24px viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Uses inline path data without metadata, title, or wrapper groups.
concerns: No inline title, role, or accessibility metadata is present; accessibility depends on the consuming context.
notes: A small reusable outline SVG for a muted volume control state.
questions: Should consumers treat this icon as decorative or provide an accessible label?
Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended icon stroke standard for this set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies a volume mute icon, and the SVG content depicts that state.
intention_signals: It appears intended as a reusable volume-off or mute icon that can inherit color from its surrounding UI.
contribution: Provides a compact, themeable audio-mute glyph composed of a speaker silhouette and crossed mute strokes in a standard 24px SVG coordinate space.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px outline SVG volume-mute icon using currentColor strokes, a speaker path, and crossed diagonal mute marks.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a muted or volume-off audio control state in a reusable icon asset.
